label: Phase 1 Diagnostic Foundations — Duplication, Scope Fence, and Phase 2 Readiness Report

duplicationReport.summary: No duplicate question stems identified across Phase 1. All 132 stem anchors are unique. The following near-collision risks were reviewed and resolved.

stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.risk: Chest pain — ACS vs thunderclap headache
stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.resolution: ACS questions (np-p1-001 through np-p1-006) focus exclusively on chest presentation, ECG patterns, and risk stratification. Thunderclap headache questions (np-p1-044) focus exclusively on SAH diagnosis via LP. No content overlap confirmed.

np-p1-071 tests vasovagal recognition via prodrome and trigger; np-p1-075 tests seizure vs syncope differential via clinical features (tonic-clonic before LOC, post-ictal state). Different anchor clinical scenarios; no stem overlap.

stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.risk: Fever — EBV (np-p1-057 in fatigue vs np-p1-065 in fever) and monospot content
stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.resolution: np-p1-057 (fatigue topic): tests which initial test confirms the mono diagnosis (monospot). np-p1-065 (fever topic): tests the amoxicillin rash phenomenon in mono — the teaching point is NOT to label it a penicillin allergy. np-p1-070 (fever topic): tests heterophile-negative mono syndrome (CMV/HIV differential). All three use unique clinical vignettes with different teaching objectives. No duplication.

stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.risk: DKA diagnosis appearing in both fatigue-differential (np-p1-054: T2DM hyperglycemia) and endocrine-crisis topics (np-p1-127 through np-p1-129)
stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.resolution: np-p1-054 (fatigue): tests identification of uncontrolled T2DM as the cause of fatigue in a new diagnosis scenario with HbA1c 9.8%. The endocrine crisis questions (np-p1-127–129) test T1DM DKA recognition (Kussmaul, anion gap, fruity breath), HHS differential, and euglycemic DKA from SGLT-2 inhibitors. Clinical scenarios, patients, and teaching points are entirely distinct. No stem overlap.

stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.risk: Thyroid content in fatigue topics vs endocrine crisis
stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.resolution: Fatigue section (np-p1-051, np-p1-052) tests recognition of hypothyroid and hyperthyroid symptom patterns and initial lab (TSH). Endocrine crisis questions (np-p1-131, np-p1-132) test thyroid storm and myxedema coma recognition in acute/crisis presentation. Non-overlapping: fatigue = outpatient lab-based recognition; endocrine crisis = acute decompensation recognition. Scope fence SF-03 (thyroid diagnosis vs levothyroxine dosing) is not tested in Phase 1 — levothyroxine dosing/monitoring content is deferred to Phase 3.

stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.risk: Lymphadenopathy — EBV in lymphadenopathy topic vs EBV in fatigue/fever topics
stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.resolution: np-p1-057 (fatigue): tests confirmatory test for EBV in a fatigued patient. np-p1-065 (fever): tests amoxicillin rash in mono. np-p1-122–126 (lymphadenopathy): tests Hodgkin, Virchow's node, sarcoidosis hilar adenopathy, PGL-HIV, CLL, cat scratch disease, and cervical abscess. EBV is not re-tested in the lymphadenopathy topic — the set covers distinct pathologies. No duplication.

stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.risk: Pyelonephritis appearing in both flank pain (np-p1-084) and fever (np-p1-069) topics
stemAnchorCollisionsReviewed.resolution: np-p1-084 (flank pain): uses CVA tenderness to distinguish pyelonephritis from uncomplicated cystitis as the teaching point — upper vs lower tract disease. np-p1-069 (fever): uses the same underlying disease but teaches disposition decision (when to admit vs treat outpatient) in a septic-appearing diabetic patient. Different clinical scenarios, different patients, different teaching objectives. Anchor phrases are distinct.

duplicationReport.verdict: PASS — No duplicate stems. All reviewed near-collisions resolve to distinct teaching objectives.

fenceReview.reason: Neither t2dm-intensification nor glp1-sglt2 topics are in Phase 1. Phase 1 diabetes content (np-p1-054, np-p1-127–129) covers only recognition of uncontrolled T2DM, DKA, HHS, and euglycemic DKA — no prescribing or agent selection content. Fence not needed in Phase 1.

fenceReview.review: Thyroid recognition questions (np-p1-051, np-p1-052) correctly stay within the 'diagnosis/recognition' boundary: they test which lab confirms the diagnosis (TSH) and which symptom pattern suggests each condition. No levothyroxine dosing, titration, monitoring intervals, or drug interactions content appears in Phase 1. COMPLIANT.
CKD anemia (np-p1-058) references EPO deficiency as the diagnosis but does not include ESA dosing, iron IV protocols, or renal medication adjustment tables — those belong to Phase 3. COMPLIANT.
MDD recognition question (np-p1-056) identifies DSM-5 criteria and PHQ-9 scoring but does not include antidepressant selection, SSRI vs SNRI comparisons, dosing, or titration. COMPLIANT.
All 132 questions reviewed against Phase 1 generation rules:

recognitionVsManagementBoundary.notes: Questions that identify a diagnosis (correct answer) stop at naming the condition, choosing the confirmatory test, or triggering the appropriate emergent referral. None prescribe specific drug doses, titration schedules, or chronic disease management protocols. One intentional exception: questions involving 'administer X immediately' (np-p1-001, np-p1-046, np-p1-099, np-p1-130, np-p1-131) are appropriate because the RECOGNITION of the emergency inherently includes knowing to initiate the critical first step (aspirin for STEMI, ceftriaxone for meningococcemia, hydrocortisone for adrenal crisis) — these are recognition-driven actions, not management protocols.

difficultyDistribution.note: Consistent with NP advanced practice framework requirement for hard/expert only

questionTypeDistribution.note: Proportions are estimates based on stem classification

phase1TopicAdjustmentsNeeded.note: 8 questions generated vs 10 target. Shortfall of 2 questions. Recommend adding: (1) a urological emergency differentiation between renal colic vs AAA in an older male, and (2) a vesicoureteral reflux recognition case in a child with recurrent UTIs and DMSA scan finding. These can be added in a targeted pass without touching other topics.

7 questions generated vs 10 target. Shortfall of 3 questions. Recommend adding: (1) rheumatoid arthritis synovitis in the knee with morning stiffness >60 min and positive RF/anti-CCP, (2) septic arthritis differentiation from crystal arthropathy using synovial fluid WBC thresholds, and (3) patellofemoral pain syndrome (runner's knee) recognition in a young female runner. These are distinct from existing stems.

7 questions generated vs 10 target. Shortfall of 3 questions. Recommend adding: (1) subconjunctival hemorrhage — benign self-limiting, no treatment needed, distinguish from serious pathology; (2) allergic conjunctivitis vs vernal keratoconjunctivitis; (3) herpes simplex keratitis — dendritic ulcer on fluorescein staining.

7 questions generated vs 10 target. Shortfall of 3 questions. Recommend adding: (1) drug-induced lymphadenopathy (phenytoin, hydralazine), (2) reactive lymphadenopathy duration-based decision to biopsy (>4–6 weeks, >1 cm, non-tender hard), (3) toxoplasma lymphadenitis in immunocompetent patient. All distinct from existing stems.

7 questions generated vs 8 target. Shortfall of 1 question. Recommend adding: granulomatosis with polyangiitis (GPA/Wegener's) presenting with hemoptysis + sinusitis + saddle nose — c-ANCA differentiation from anti-GBM (which is covered in np-p1-119).

phase1ReadinessForPhase2.overallVerdict: READY TO PROCEED TO PHASE 2 with targeted shortfall questions added
phase2PrerequisitesMet: All 14 Phase 1 diagnostic foundation topics are covered with lesson outlines
Critical recognition questions for life-threatening conditions are present for all major presentations
Scope fences defined and verified compliant — ready to govern Phase 2 prescribing content

scopeFencesPhase2Required: SF-01 will activate: t2dm-intensification-beyond-metformin vs glp1-sglt2-comorbidity-selection
SF-02 will activate: thyroid-disease-diagnosis-management vs levothyroxine-dosing-monitoring
SF-03 will activate: ckd-progression-monitoring-medication vs medication-dose-adjustment-ckd
SF-04 will activate: mdd-diagnosis-risk-initial-management vs antidepressant-selection-ssri-snri
additionalScopeFencesNeeded: Phase 2 should define a new fence between: hypertension-diagnosis-staging vs antihypertensive-class-selection — high overlap risk identified in the blueprint
Phase 2 should define a new fence between: heart-failure-phenotype-diagnosis vs heart-failure-pharmacotherapy — EF-based drug selection belongs to management, not recognition

phase1ReadinessForPhase2.phase2ExecutionOrder: Per np-advanced-practice-blueprint.json audit, Phase 2 covers: Prescribing & Advanced Pharmacology (antihypertensives, antibiotics, pain management, psychiatric prescribing). These should follow the same pattern: lesson outlines first, then question generation, then scope fence verification.

Question sets for topics 8–14 (flank pain through endocrine crisis), 51 questions
fileManifest.status: COMPLETE — shortfall noted in flank pain, knee, red eye, lymphadenopathy, hemoptysis (–12 questions from target)
